mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy shift that lowers the cost of capital, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency in signal months is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0% from below, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal's t-statistic against zero is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series increase together.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly increase coincident with INDPRO monthly increase
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and INDPRO increase, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time, or the joint signal underperforms the standalone RSAFS trend signal by more than 2 annualized percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices accelerates the expected path of monetary tightening, but this relationship generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days only when the federal funds rate is already above 2.0%, reflecting a constrained central bank response function.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC below -0.03, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not statistically different from the unconditional Sharpe (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that immediately reduces near-term earnings optimism, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of months when RSAFS declines, or the mean return in those months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates reduced manufacturing output and corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months following an INDPRO decline, or the signal's information coefficient is positive in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling payroll employment combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary impulse where labor market weakness coincides with tighter financial conditions, producing strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly decline and FEDFUNDS monthly increase
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of PAYEMS decline and FEDFUNDS increase fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power over individual series in a regression (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly real GDP growth signals an economic contraction that depresses aggregate corporate revenues and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a negative GDPC1 print, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
